differential_diagnoses: Ectopic variceal bleeding (anastomotic varices at hepaticojejunostomy)
Chronic portal vein thrombosis with portal hypertension
next_steps: Selective visceral angiography (celiac and mesenteric) to identify arterial pseudoaneurysms or vascular-biliary fistulas
Percutaneous transhepatic cholangiography (PTC) for direct visualization of the biliary tree and potential diagnostic access
Interventional Radiology consultation for evaluation of portal venous anatomy and potential decompression via TIPS or embolization of collaterals
Multidisciplinary review involving Hepatobiliary Surgery and Interventional Radiology
Continue supportive care with iron and blood transfusions as needed until bleeding source is hemodynamically localized
